subject: Chemistry
solution:
H2O2 decomposes slowly on exposure to light.
2H2O2l→2H2Ol+O2g
In the presence of metal surfaces or traces of alkali (present in glass containers), the above reaction is catalysed. It is, therefore, stored in wax-lined glass or plastic vessels in dark. Urea can be added as a stabiliser. It is kept away from dust because dust can induce explosive decomposition of the compound. Urea act as negative catalysts in the decomposition of hydrogen peroxide.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Isotopes are the atoms of the same element with different mass number. They have the same atomic number. So, number of protons and electrons are same, Hence their electronic configuration are same, but number of neutrons are different.

Hydrogen has three isotopes: protium, H11, deuterium, H12 or D and tritium, H13 or T.  These isotopes differ from one another in respect of the presence of neutrons. Ordinary hydrogen, protium, has no neutrons, deuterium (also known as heavy hydrogen) has one and tritium has two neutrons in the nucleus.

Statement-2 : Bond energy of O-H< Bond energy of O-D.
So rate of reaction of cleavage of O-H bond is faster than that of O-D bond.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
PbS+4H2O2→PbSO4+4H2O

In the above reaction lead sulphide oxidised to lead sulphate. Hence, hydrogen peroxide acts as oxidising agent.

2Fe2++H2O2→2Fe3++2OH-

In the above reaction Fe2+ oxidised to Fe3+. Hence, hydrogen peroxide acts as oxidising agent.

Mn2++H2O2→Mn4++2OH-

In the above reaction Mn2+ oxidised to Mn4+. Hence, hydrogen peroxide acts as oxidising agent.

HOCl+H2O2→H3O++Cl-1+O2

In the above reaction Cl+1 reduced to Cl-, hence, hydrogen peroxide acts as a reducing agent.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
In this method calculated amount of lime is added to hard water. It precipitates out calcium carbonate and magnesium hydroxide which can be filtered off In Clark's method lime water is used.

It is because of low solubility product of Mg(OH)2 as compared to that of MgCO3.

CaHCO32+2CaOH2→2CaCO3+2H2O

MgHCO32+2CaOH2→2CaCO3+MgOH2+2H2O

solution:
Oxidizing nature of H2â€‹O2â€‹ means it oxidizes other substance and itself gets reduced. In such reactions O2â€‹ is not evolved. 

2I-+H2O2+2H+→I2+2H2O

Oxygen reduces from -1 to -2

So, its reduction will takes place. Hence it will behave as oxidising agent or it shows oxidising nature. While in other option it change from (-1) to 0.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Carbon monoxide does not react with water to form an acid.

The carbon monoxide molecule is one that won't react with water at all. As the acidic oxide has a higher oxidation state, it reacts with water to produce acid, and reacts with base to produce salt. 

CO2+H2O→H2CO3

C+H2Osteam→CO+H2

CH4+H2O→CO+3H2C3H8+H2O→3CO+H2 both reactions are carried out at 1270 K temp. with Ni catalyst

solution:
In group 15

]PN→ Non-metal

]SbAs→ Metalloid

Bi]→ Metal

Hydrides of group 15 elements are

NH3

PH3

AsH3

SbH3

BiH3

In NH3, hydrogen atom gets partial positive charge due to less electronegativity. But in BiH3, hydrogen atom gets partial negative charge because hydrogen is more electronegative than bismuth.

i.e. BiH3 is a strong reducing agent than others because we know that H- is a strong reducing agent.

In this reaction, H2O2 acts as oxidising agent.

(b) The basic principle of hydrogen economy is the transportation and storage of energy in the form of liquids or gaseous dihydrogen.

Advantage of hydrogen economy is that energy is transmitted in the form of dihydrogen and not as electric power

solution:
H2O2 is used in the synthesis of hydroquinone, tartaric acid and certain food products and pharmaceuticals (cephalosporin).

It is increasingly being used in environmental chemistry to control pollution by treatment of domestic and industrial effluents, oxidation of cyanide, and restoration of aerobic conditions to sewage waste.

subject: Chemistry
solution: H2O2 decomposes on exposure to sunlight or on heating. It also decomposes on standing but at a slow rate. These reactions occur explosively when H2O2 is in contact with dust. Urea acts as a negative catalyst to this reaction and slow down the decomposition of hydrogen peroxide.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Peroxodisulphate, obtained by electrolytic oxidation of acidified sulphate solutions at high current density, on hydrolysis yields hydrogen peroxide.

2HSO4- (aq) →Electrolysis HO3SOOSO3H (aq) →Electrolysis 2HSO4- (aq) + 2H+ (aq) + H2O2 (aq)

This method is now used for the laboratory preparation of D2O2.

K2 S2O8 (s)+2D2O (l)→2KDSO4 (aq.)+D2O2 (l)

Hard water is water that has high mineral content. Hard water is formed when water percolates through deposits of limestone, chalk or gypsum which are largely made up of calcium and magnesium carbonates, bicarbonates and sulfates.
